name: StatsForecast (Nixtla)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); Nixtla project; no API costs

performance.notes: Up to 100x faster than statsmodels for same algorithms; Numba/Cython optimized; parallel processing across series

agent_readiness.notes: Nixtla's high-performance statistical forecasting library. 100x faster than statsmodels for ARIMA, ETS, Theta, AutoARIMA. Cross-sectional parallel forecasting for thousands of series. Ray/Dask distributed backends. AutoARIMA, AutoETS, AutoTheta with automatic model selection. Prediction intervals. Integrates with NeuralForecast for hybrid statistical+DL pipelines. Used in M competitions.

gotchas: Statistical models only — no deep learning (use NeuralForecast for DL)
Requires long history for ARIMA/SARIMA to work well
AutoARIMA computationally expensive for very long series
best_when: Forecasting thousands of time series efficiently (retail demand, supply chain)
Replacing statsmodels for production statistical forecasting pipelines
M competition-style benchmarks needing ARIMA/ETS accuracy at scale
Hybrid pipelines combining statistical baselines with neural networks
summary: StatsForecast is Nixtla's high-performance statistical forecasting library, 100x faster than statsmodels for ARIMA, ETS, and Theta models. Supports parallel forecasting across thousands of series. AutoARIMA and AutoETS for automatic model selection. Pairs with NeuralForecast for hybrid pipelines. Used in M competitions. Best for production forecasting of many series at scale.
